quiz.title: Unit 6 Quiz 10, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Reaction Types and Balancing Equations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Identifying Reaction Types (Single Displacement, Double Displacement, Decomposition, Neutralization, Combustion), Balancing Equations

questions.question:
$\text{MgCl}_2 + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow 2\text{AgCl} + \text{Mg(NO}_3)_2$

What type of reaction is this?
questions.explanation: This is a double displacement reaction where the cations and anions exchange partners. Magnesium and silver switch places, forming silver chloride precipitate and magnesium nitrate.

questions.question:
$\text{Pb(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{KI} \rightarrow \text{PbI}_2 + 2\text{KNO}_3$

This reaction is classified as:
questions.explanation: This is a double displacement reaction. Lead and potassium exchange partners, forming lead(II) iodide (yellow precipitate) and potassium nitrate.

questions.question:
$\text{Zn} + \text{Cu(NO}_3)_2 \rightarrow \text{Zn(NO}_3)_2 + \text{Cu}$

This reaction is an example of:
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement reaction. Zinc displaces copper from copper nitrate because zinc is more reactive than copper.

questions.question:
$\text{Ca} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{Ca(OH)}_2 + \text{H}_2$

The type of reaction is:
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement reaction. Calcium displaces hydrogen from water, forming calcium hydroxide and hydrogen gas.

questions.question:
$2\text{NaHCO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{CO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O} + \text{CO}_2$

This reaction is classified as:
questions.explanation: This is a decomposition reaction. A single compound (sodium bicarbonate) breaks down into multiple products (sodium carbonate, water, and carbon dioxide).

questions.question:
$\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{NaOH} \rightarrow \text{Na}_2\text{SO}_4 + 2\text{H}_2\text{O}$

The reaction type is:
questions.explanation: This is an acid-base neutralization reaction. Sulfuric acid reacts with sodium hydroxide to produce a salt (sodium sulfate) and water.

questions.question:
$\text{HNO}_3 + \text{KOH} \rightarrow \text{KNO}_3 + \text{H}_2\text{O}$

This is an example of:
questions.explanation: This is an acid-base neutralization reaction. Nitric acid reacts with potassium hydroxide to form potassium nitrate and water.

questions.question:
$\text{HF} + \text{KOH} \rightarrow \text{KF} + \text{H}_2\text{O}$

The reaction type is:
questions.explanation: This is an acid-base neutralization reaction. Hydrofluoric acid reacts with potassium hydroxide to form potassium fluoride and water.

questions.question:
$\text{Zn} + 2\text{AgNO}_3 \rightarrow \text{Zn(NO}_3)_2 + 2\text{Ag}$

The reaction is:
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement reaction. Zinc displaces silver from silver nitrate because zinc is more reactive than silver.

questions.question:
$\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 3\text{H}_2\text{SO}_4 \rightarrow 3\text{CaSO}_4 + 2\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$

The type of reaction is:
questions.explanation: This is a double displacement reaction. The cations and anions exchange partners: calcium from calcium phosphate combines with sulfate from sulfuric acid to form calcium sulfate, while hydrogen from sulfuric acid combines with phosphate to form phosphoric acid. This is also an acid-base reaction (phosphoric acid is produced), but double displacement is the primary classification.

questions.question:
$2\text{NaCl} + 2\text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow 2\text{NaOH} + \text{H}_2 + \text{Cl}_2$

The reaction type is:
questions.explanation: This is a decomposition reaction (specifically electrolysis). Although it has two reactants, it's considered a decomposition because an electric current breaks down sodium chloride and water into their component elements and sodium hydroxide. It is not a single displacement (no single element replaces another), not double displacement (no exchange of partners), and not combustion (no oxygen involved as a reactant).

questions.question:
$\text{Fe}_2\text{O}_3 + 2\text{Al} \rightarrow 2\text{Fe} + \text{Al}_2\text{O}_3$

The type of reaction is:
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement reaction (also called the thermite reaction). Aluminum displaces iron from iron(III) oxide, forming aluminum oxide and iron metal.

questions.question:
$\text{TiCl}_4 + 2\text{Mg} \rightarrow \text{Ti} + 2\text{MgCl}_2$

This reaction is classified as:
questions.explanation: This is a single displacement reaction. Magnesium displaces titanium from titanium(IV) chloride in the Kroll process, producing titanium metal and magnesium chloride.

questions.question:
$\text{CS}_2 + 3\text{O}_2 \rightarrow \text{CO}_2 + 2\text{SO}_2$

This reaction is __________
questions.explanation: This is a combustion reaction (carbon disulfide burning in oxygen). Combustion reactions are always exothermic, releasing heat and often light. The formation of stable products like CO₂ and SO₂ from elements releases energy. Therefore, this reaction is exothermic.

questions.question:
___ $\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4$ + ___ $\text{Ca(OH)}_2$ → ___ $\text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2$ + ___ $\text{H}_2\text{O}$

What are the correct coefficients for the balanced equation?
questions.explanation: $2\text{H}_3\text{PO}_4 + 3\text{Ca(OH)}_2 \rightarrow \text{Ca}_3(\text{PO}_4)_2 + 6\text{H}_2\text{O}$ is balanced. This is an acid-base neutralization reaction.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered identifying reaction types and balancing equations! 🧪🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review the differences between single and double displacement reactions.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on recognizing neutralization and decomposition reactions.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the main reaction types and practice balancing chemical equations.
